creator: i+fbsd@amlegion.org
text:
(In reply to i+fbsd from comment #2)

Notes:

Originally attempted build with poudriere with a limiter of 4GB RAM per poudriere jail. This was on a VM running under bhyve with 8GB total RAM and 2 cores.

This machine profile failed to build sbconcurrency tests (multiple-producers-multiple-consumers, single-producer-multiple-consumers).

--

Changed VM to have 4 cores, remaining at 8GB of RAM and allowed poudriere 8GB of RAM per jail. It would seem that the concurrency tests generate more load than what a 2 core VM could handle (perhaps bare metal it'd have been fine?). Regardless, this configuration worked and SBCL was successfully built with threading enabled.

So, solution is throw more cores at it? Not run in VM? Or does upstream need to par back the concurrency tests? 

For me, it's solved, but no idea if "throw more cores at it" is a proper solution.
